    plastic pollution causes extensive damage to marine life and compromises ecosystem stability through multiple pathways. marine mammals and seabirds often consume plastic mistaking it for food sources which results in internal injuries. entanglement in discarded fishing gear prevents animals from hunting successfully and leads to painful suffocation underwater. microplastics break down into tiny particles absorbing harmful toxins that bioaccumulate in predator organs. coral reefs suffer from physical smothering when heavy debris settles upon sensitive colonial structures. nutrient imbalances occur in sediment layers when plastics decompose releasing hazardous additives into the water. fisheries yield reduced catch volumes because contaminated stocks are unsafe for human consumption markets. conservationists urge governments to enforce stricter waste management policies to mitigate the ongoing threat.
